sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 32-year-old female with a history of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) with symptoms of functional abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting.
She was previously diagnosed in August 2019 and treated with Bentyl.
She had a prescription for Reglan, which she used as needed for nausea, but was instructed to stop using it and use Zofran instead.
She was also instructed to start a low-dose fiber supplement, probiotics, and Bentyl up to four times daily for intestinal cramping.
The patient reports that her symptoms have flared up due to eating spicy foods, such as spicy shrimp, and drinking alcohol.
She experiences cramping and sharp pains in the lower abdomen, and has had diarrhea after eating out on Friday and Saturday nights.
She reports that her bowel movements are usually formed, but she experiences diarrhea when she eats out.
She also reports that fried chicken and pasta with Alfredo sauce, which she normally doesn't have trouble with, have caused her symptoms to flare up recently.
She drinks tequila or dark liquor on Fridays and Saturdays, and has tried drinking water instead of soda, which has helped.
She has been taking Bentyl as needed, but has not been taking Cetrucel as instructed.
She has been avoiding heavy foods, but still experiences cramping and sharp pains.
She is concerned about not being able to go out without eating fried chicken.
She denies any tenderness in the abdomen, and her physical exam is unremarkable.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of IBS.
Review of systems: The patient reports symptoms of diarrhea, nausea, and abdominal pain.
Current medications: Bentyl 20 milligrams as needed 30-45 minutes prior to eating out; Zofran as needed; Cetrucel 1 tablet twice daily for regularity.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's abdomen is soft, non-tender, with normal active bowel sounds.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has IBS with symptoms of functional abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting, aggravated by high stress, poor eating habits, and poor sleeping habits.
Problem list: IBS; Active; IBS with symptoms of functional abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting; Active.
Plan:
I prescribed Cetrucel 1 tablet twice daily for regularity.
I instructed the patient to use Bentyl 20 milligrams as needed 30-45 minutes prior to eating out at a restaurant.
I instructed the patient to use Zofran if needed, but encouraged her to reduce greasy and fatty foods and alcohol consumption.
I also instructed the patient to start taking Elavil 25 milligrams at bedtime for IBS.
Follow-up:
I instructed the patient to follow up in one month by emailing over MyChart regarding any side effects and issues, or issues with Elavil or if adjustments are needed.
